creator: esk@ira.uka.de
text:
[Glenn Johnson]
> On Fri, Oct 06, 2000 at 07:24:09PM +0200, esk@ira.uka.de wrote:
>>> Description:
>> 

>> RealNetworks has released a new version of the realplayer (i.e.,
>> the old distribution is no longer available).  The makefile needs
>> to be updated to reflect this.

> I think I see the problem.  The Makefile refers people to the
> following URL:

> http://www.real.com/player/7/

> I think it should refer them to the following URL:

> http://scopes.real.com/real/player/unix/unix.html?src=rpbform

Yes.  You're right.  The question, of course, is whether one wants to
use the Community Supported player or the player which is supported by 
RealNetworks.  I must say that I don't really know what the
differences between the two of them are.

	eSk
